section.para: The act of content moderation on social media is far more than a simple filtering mechanism. It is a sophisticated system of mechanized creative control that directs and shapes the IP created by users to serve the platform's commercial interests. When viewed through the copyright principles of AI-assisted authorship, the platform emerges not as a passive host but as an active "executive author," transforming the raw creative output of its users into a curated, monetizable product.

This level of creative control and transformation of value fundamentally alters the legal nature of the relationship. Under California law, it provides powerful evidence that the user is not a mere licensor, but a service provider in a misclassified employment relationship. The very AI systems designed to protect the platform's business model may, in fact, be creating its single greatest legal liability.
